title: heap-use-after-free in RESTORE when a module sets key metadata during keyspace notification
category: re-entrancy use-after-free (a module notification callback reallocates the object the caller still holds)
crash_site: heap-use-after-free reading the restored object's type field after a RESTORE, surfacing in restoreCommand (src/cluster.c). The read is correct; the object was reallocated underneath it.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: kvobjSet (actually reallocates/frees the kvobj — looks like the culprit but is correct) and RedisModule_SetKeyMeta inside the module callback. The fix lands in restoreCommand (cache the field before the notification).
ground_truth.fix_summary: restoreCommand held a local kv pointer across notifyKeyspaceEvent(). If a module's notification callback calls RedisModule_SetKeyMeta for the first time, kvobjSet reallocates the kvobj (to add a metadata slot) and frees the old one, so the local kv dangles and the subsequent kv->type read is a UAF. Fix: save kv->type to a local before the notification calls.

admission.note: Authored from PR #14929 body+diff. crash site == fix fn (restoreCommand) -> moderate C1, BUT the structural difficulty is the re-entrancy: the realloc/free happens deep inside a MODULE notification callback (notifyKeyspaceEvent -> module -> RedisModule_SetKeyMeta -> kvobjSet), far from restoreCommand. Decoy = kvobjSet (the visible free). Tool value = tracing the notification re-entry edge. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/redis/redis/pull/14929 — heap-use-after-free in restoreCommand when module sets key metadata during notification; fix 40c140bf16ca, base bbc0dcbb9af7.
